film_stock_groups.name: Blue-Sensitive (1850s–1900s)
film_stock_groups.description: Historical emulsions sensitive only to UV and Blue light.

stocks.name: Wet Plate Collodion (~1860s)
stocks.description: UV through blue only. The Civil War era look.

stocks.name: Eastman 2366 Silver Nitrate
stocks.description: Modern blue-sensitive film. Falls off a cliff in blue-green.

film_stock_groups.name: Orthochromatic (1873–1930s)
film_stock_groups.description: Sensitive to Blue and Green. Blind to Red.

stocks.name: Generic Ortho (~1920s)
stocks.description: Typical silent-era film stock. Dark lips, lighter skin.

stocks.name: Reference Ortho
stocks.description: Orthochromatic: Completely blind to Red light.

stocks.name: Ilford / Ortho Plus
stocks.description: Modern orthochromatic. Can be handled under red safelight.

film_stock_groups.name: Panchromatic Classic (1930s–1980s)
film_stock_groups.description: The Golden Age of B&W. Full spectrum sensitivity.

stocks.name: Agfa / Isopan Record
stocks.description: Classic ultra-high speed. Vintage low-light look with pronounced character.

stocks.name: Agfa / 200X
stocks.description: Low red weight; blue-biased pan.

stocks.name: Agfa / APX / 25
stocks.description: Fine grain; more balanced.

stocks.name: Agfa / APX / 100
stocks.description: General purpose Agfa emulsion.

stocks.name: Agfa / APX / 400
stocks.description: High speed Agfa.

stocks.name: Fuji / Neopan 400 Professional
stocks.description: Traditional grain. Sharp, punchy contrast, and clean highlights.

stocks.name: Fuji / Neopan 1600 Super Presto
stocks.description: Iconic high-speed film. Heavy grain, deep blacks, high contrast.

stocks.name: Kodak / Super-X (1930s)
stocks.description: Vintage Pan (Type B): Reduced red sensitivity.

stocks.name: Kodak / Panatomic-X
stocks.description: Fine Grain Pan: Very balanced, slight blue bias.

stocks.name: Kodak / Plus-X 125
stocks.description: Balanced, good red sensitivity.

stocks.name: Kodak / Tri-X 400
stocks.description: Iconic photojournalism film. Lifted shadows in blue light.

stocks.name: Kodak / Double-X 5222
stocks.description: Motion picture stock. Nearly identical to Tri-X.

stocks.name: Ilford / FP4 Plus
stocks.description: Higher red sensitivity relative to other Ilfords.

stocks.name: Ilford / HP5 Plus
stocks.description: Blue-weighted panchromatic. Atmospheric street photography.

stocks.name: Ilford / Pan F Plus
stocks.description: Highest red weight among classic Ilfords.

film_stock_groups.name: Modern T-Grain (1980s–Present)
film_stock_groups.description: Tabular crystals for sharper, smoother tone.

stocks.name: Kodak / T-Max / 100
stocks.description: Orthopanchromatic look with relatively lower red.

stocks.name: Kodak / T-Max / 400
stocks.description: Slightly more red sensitivity than TMX-100.

stocks.name: Ilford / Delta / 100
stocks.description: Green-dominant; smooth tonality.

stocks.name: Ilford / Delta / 400
stocks.description: Very similar spectral response to Delta 100.

stocks.name: Ilford / Delta / 3200
stocks.description: Higher red weight for push applications.

stocks.name: Ilford / XP2 Super
stocks.description: C-41 process chromogenic B&W film.

stocks.name: Fuji / Neopan Acros 100 II
stocks.description: Distinctive 'Acros look'; valley at ~500nm.

stocks.name: Adox CHS 100
stocks.description: Reduced red sensitivity.

film_stock_groups.name: Extended Red / Near-IR
film_stock_groups.description: Enhanced red sensitivity. Wood effect capable (with filters).

stocks.name: Ilford / SFX 200
stocks.description: Sensitivity extended to ~740 nm.

stocks.name: Rollei Infrared 400
stocks.description: Sensitivity extended to ~820 nm.

stocks.name: ITU-R BT.709 Luminance
stocks.description: Human photopic vision weighting. Heavily green dominant.

stocks.name: Equal Weight
stocks.description: Simple average. standard desaturation.
